top_categories.workout_context: When someone attacks on mountain passes, you have 10 seconds to respond or you're off the back. This builds that response.
Race-pace efforts that mimic UCI Gran Fondo Loutraki's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before November.
69 miles on mountain passes burns energy faster than the distance suggests. Train the fade resistance now.
UCI Gran Fondo Loutraki's mountain passes forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 69 miles.
UCI Gran Fondo Loutraki's mountain passes rewards steady threshold power. Surging and recovering costs more watts than just holding.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of UCI Gran Fondo Loutraki. Train it until it feels boring.
Endurance with intensity spikes baked in. Exactly what 3,281ft of climbing on mountain passes does to your power file on race day.
Short, max efforts are unavoidable. Punchy climbs, gap closures, attacks on mountain passes. If you haven't trained them, you'll crack.
Base fitness for UCI Gran Fondo Loutraki. Without this, the hard workouts break you down instead of building you up.
At UCI Gran Fondo Loutraki, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
Double-threshold training builds sustained power with less fatigue. More FTP per hour of training.
How long can you hold 105% FTP before you blow? At UCI Gran Fondo Loutraki, that number decides whether you bridge or get dropped.

race_overlay.nutrition: Target 40–60g carbs/hour for UCI Gran Fondo Loutraki’s 69 miles. Front-load calories in the first half. One bottle per hour minimum, more in heat.
race_overlay.altitude: Moderate altitude at UCI Gran Fondo Loutraki. Arrive 2–3 days early. Reduce intensity expectations by 5–10%. Hydrate aggressively.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on VO2max, Race Simulation, and Durability to prepare you for 69 miles of mountain passes in Loutraki, Isthmus of Corinth, Greece.
